$comment: Release self-declaration consumed by the unified registry's auto-core-release resolver (sh2-plugin-registry/scripts/resolve-core-candidate.mjs) at the git tag being released. The supports.* semver ranges decide whether a tagged core can be auto-matched with the latest published counterparts into a signed release candidate PR. Keep pluginApiVersion in sync with config/services.yaml (CI enforces it) and widen/bump supports.frontend deliberately - while the platform is pre-1.0, every 0.x minor is breaking. Raise supports.frontend's lower bound whenever core changes/removes an API contract the frontend depends on: the floor 0.1.29 is the frontend release that adopts the field-naming unification - migration Version20260622165615 drops the `shared_` prefix from 47 style props (`shared_color`->`color`, `shared_variant`->`variant`, `shared_radius`->`radius`, ...; reserved `shared_height`/`shared_width`/`shared_icon` keep their prefix), so this core emits the unprefixed names that a 0.1.23-0.1.28 frontend does not read. That 0.1.29 floor also still carries everything the previous 0.1.23 floor required - the full 0.1.15 style-schema contract (mobile-rendering taxonomy: style `renderTarget`, required field `scope`, the shared_/web_ field split with `pages.id_platform` removed in favour of the page access type) PLUS the alert/badge/avatar/button/login authoring-polish wave (alert `web_with_close_button` renamed to common `closable`; button/badge promoted to `variant`; badge `circle`; avatar `name`; login `subtitle`) - so an older frontend that still reads any pre-rename field name can never be auto-assembled with this core into a release.
